# inkaR: Access the INKAR Database of the BBSR

The inkaR package provides a user-friendly interface to access, search,
and download statistical indicators from the INKAR (Indikatoren und
Karten zur Raum- und Stadtentwicklung) database provided by the Federal
Institute for Research on Building, Urban Affairs and Spatial
Development (BBSR).

## Main Functions

- [`view_indicators()`](https://ofurkancoban.github.io/inkaR/reference/view_indicators.md):
  Opens the list of available indicators in a viewer. Supports German
  ("de") and English ("en") modes. Sorted by active status.

- [`get_inkar_data()`](https://ofurkancoban.github.io/inkaR/reference/get_inkar_data.md):
  API wrapper to fetch data for a specific indicator. Supports
  auto-saving to CSV (`csv = TRUE`).

- [`get_geographies()`](https://ofurkancoban.github.io/inkaR/reference/get_geographies.md):
  Helper to list available spatial levels (e.g., Kreise, Gemeinden) or
  regions within a level.

- [`search_indicators()`](https://ofurkancoban.github.io/inkaR/reference/search_indicators.md):
  Search for indicators by text pattern.

## Usage Workflow

1.  **Explore**: Use
    [`view_indicators()`](https://ofurkancoban.github.io/inkaR/reference/view_indicators.md)
    to find the `Shortname` (e.g., "001") or `M_ID` of the desired
    indicator.

2.  **Download**: Use `get_inkar_data("001", level="KRE", year=2021)` or
    ranges (`year=2010:2020`) to fetch the data.

3.  **Export**: Add `csv = TRUE` to `get_inkar_data` to save the result
    immediately.

## See also

Useful links:

- <https://github.com/ofurkancoban/inkaR>

- Report bugs at <https://github.com/ofurkancoban/inkaR/issues>

## Author

**Maintainer**: Omer Furkan Coban <oemer.furkan.coban@uni-oldenburg.de>
([ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0005-3623-7178))

## Examples

``` r
if (interactive()) {
  # 1. View available indicators (German)
  view_indicators()

  # 2. View in English
  view_indicators("en")
}

# \donttest{
  # 3. Search for "GDP" (Bruttoinlandsprodukt)
  try(search_indicators("GDP", lang = "en"))
#> No indicators found for: 'GDP'

  # 4. Download data for GDP (011) for Districts (KRE)
  #    Note: You can use Shortnames ("011"), numeric M_IDs (11), or simple codes ("bip")
  try(data <- get_inkar_data("011", level = "KRE", year = 2021, lang = "de", csv = FALSE))
#> Using M_ID '11' directly.
#> ✔ Downloaded: Bruttoinlandsprodukt (BIP) absolut in Millionen Euro [1.000 Euro]
#>   Level: Kreise | Regions: 400 | Year(s): 2021 | Rows: 400

  # 5. Download and save directly as CSV
  try(get_inkar_data("011", csv = TRUE, export_dir = tempdir()))
#> Using M_ID '11' directly.
#> Data saved to: /var/folders/b0/j94ythbx05l0_j9ym9pnrs4w0000gn/T//Rtmp4qAWoY/inkar_11_KRE_Bruttoinlandsprodukt_BIP_absolut_in_Millionen_Euro.csv
#> ✔ Downloaded: Bruttoinlandsprodukt (BIP) absolut in Millionen Euro [1.000 Euro]
#>   Level: Kreise | Regions: 400 | Year(s): 1995–2022 | Rows: 11200
#>       Kennziffer                                  Raumeinheit Aggregat M_ID
```
